evals.prompt: Build a Flutter product listing screen with Riverpod for state management. It should fetch products from a REST API, show loading shimmer, handle errors with retry, support pull-to-refresh, and have a search bar that filters products locally. Use clean architecture layers.
evals.expected_output: Flutter screen with Riverpod AsyncNotifier, repository pattern, loading/error/empty states, pull-to-refresh, local search filter

assertions.name: clean_architecture
assertions.description: Code organized into data/domain/presentation layers

assertions.name: riverpod_state
assertions.description: Uses Riverpod provider (AsyncNotifier or FutureProvider) for state

assertions.name: loading_error_empty
assertions.description: All three states handled with appropriate widgets

assertions.name: pull_to_refresh
assertions.description: RefreshIndicator wrapping the list for pull-to-refresh

assertions.name: search_filter
assertions.description: Search bar filters products without re-fetching from API

evals.prompt: Implement a responsive Flutter layout that shows a list on mobile, master-detail on tablet, and a three-column layout on desktop. Use LayoutBuilder for breakpoints. The content is a list of conversations (like a chat app).
evals.expected_output: Responsive layout using LayoutBuilder with 3 breakpoints, adaptive navigation, shared state across panels

assertions.name: layout_builder
assertions.description: Uses LayoutBuilder or MediaQuery for breakpoint detection

assertions.name: three_breakpoints
assertions.description: Mobile (<768), tablet (768-1024), desktop (>1024) layouts

assertions.name: master_detail
assertions.description: Tablet shows list + detail side by side

assertions.name: shared_state
assertions.description: Selected conversation syncs across layout variants

assertions.name: no_hardcoded_sizes
assertions.description: No fixed pixel widths — uses Flexible, Expanded, or fractions

evals.prompt: Create a Flutter form with multiple field types: text input, email with validation, password with strength indicator, dropdown select, date picker, and a photo upload. Use a form key for validation and show inline errors.
evals.expected_output: Flutter Form widget with GlobalKey, TextFormField validators, custom widgets for password strength and photo upload

assertions.name: form_key
assertions.description: Uses GlobalKey<FormState> for form validation

assertions.name: field_validators
assertions.description: Each field has a validator function with error messages

assertions.name: inline_errors
assertions.description: Validation errors shown below each field

assertions.name: password_strength
assertions.description: Visual password strength indicator

assertions.name: photo_upload
assertions.description: Image picker integration with preview

assertions.name: dispose_controllers
assertions.description: TextEditingControllers disposed in dispose()

evals.prompt: Build a Flutter bottom navigation app with 4 tabs: Home, Search, Notifications, Profile. Each tab should maintain its scroll position and state when switching. Use go_router for navigation with nested routes per tab.
evals.expected_output: Bottom nav with go_router StatefulShellRoute, preserved scroll state per tab, proper back button handling

assertions.name: go_router_navigation
assertions.description: Uses go_router with StatefulShellRoute or equivalent for tab navigation

assertions.name: four_tabs
assertions.description: Four bottom navigation tabs with icons and labels

assertions.name: state_preservation
assertions.description: Scroll position and state preserved when switching tabs

assertions.name: nested_routes
assertions.description: Each tab has its own route hierarchy

assertions.name: back_button
assertions.description: Android back button works correctly within and across tabs

evals.prompt: Implement offline-first data sync in Flutter. Use Hive for local storage, Dio for API calls, and a connectivity listener. When offline, read from cache. When back online, sync pending writes. Show a connectivity banner.
evals.expected_output: Offline-first architecture with Hive cache, Dio interceptors, connectivity listener, sync queue, and UI indicator

assertions.name: hive_local_storage
assertions.description: Uses Hive for local caching of API data

assertions.name: offline_read
assertions.description: Reads from cache when network is unavailable

assertions.name: sync_queue
assertions.description: Queues writes when offline and replays on reconnection

assertions.name: connectivity_listener
assertions.description: Listens for network state changes

assertions.name: ui_indicator
assertions.description: Shows offline/online status banner in the UI
